c.d: The potential number of viewers that can be reached through the platform.
c.s: Various sources depending on platform. e.g., Streams Charts, YouTube Analytics, Facebook Insights

c.d: The availability and variety of methods for creators to earn revenue from their live streams (e.g., ads, subscriptions, donations).
How user-friendly the platform is for both beginners and experienced streamers.
The types of tools available for engaging with the audience in real-time (e.g., chat, polls, Q&A).
The platform's primary focus and the types of content that perform best on it.
The maximum resolution and frame rate supported by the platform.
The ability to stream to multiple platforms simultaneously.
The ability to record live streams.
Number of streams that can be watched at the same time.
